title: OOF Rank-Average Blend With Beta/Sigmoid Calibration
summary: If you add a ranking model, a survival model, and a categorical specialist, the main remaining lift is likely in how they are combined.
rationale: If you add a ranking model, a survival model, and a categorical specialist, the main remaining lift is likely in how they are combined. Scikit-learn notes sigmoid calibration preserves ranking while isotonic can change AUC via ties, and beta calibration is a stronger parametric alternative to plain Platt scaling on skewed score distributions.
implementation_hint: Generate out-of-fold predictions from heterogeneous base models, combine them by rank-average or a tiny held-out logistic stack, then calibrate only on disjoint OOF predictions using beta calibration or sigmoid calibration. Prefer this over isotonic if public-LB ROC AUC stability matters more than perfect probability calibration.
expected_effect: Usually a small but durable gain from diversity, with better CV-to-LB stability than uncalibrated averaging or isotonic-heavy postprocessing.
risk: A second-stage blender/calibrator can overfit if folds are not strictly disjoint by race or if the base models are too correlated to add real diversity.
